name: Container and Package Registry Management
description: Create Artifact Registry repositories, push and pull container images, and manage npm and Python packages in a private registry.

steps.title: Enable the Artifact Registry API
steps.description: Activates the Artifact Registry API for the project.

steps.title: Create a Docker repository
steps.command: gcloud artifacts repositories create my-docker-repo --repository-format docker --location us-central1 --description "Docker images for my apps"
steps.description: Creates a Docker repository in the specified region.

steps.title: Configure Docker authentication
steps.description: Registers the Artifact Registry domain with Docker so you can push and pull images.

steps.title: Tag and push an image
steps.description: Tags a local image with the Artifact Registry path and pushes it.

steps.title: List images in the repository
steps.command: gcloud artifacts docker images list us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project-id/my-docker-repo --format "table(package, version, createTime)"
steps.description: Shows all images in the repository with version tags and creation dates.

fullScript:
# Artifact Registry management
gcloud services enable artifactregistry.googleapis.com
gcloud artifacts repositories create my-docker-repo --repository-format docker --location us-central1 --description "Docker images for my apps"
gcloud auth configure-docker us-central1-docker.pkg.dev
docker tag myapp:latest us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project-id/my-docker-repo/myapp:v1.0
docker push us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project-id/my-docker-repo/myapp:v1.0
gcloud artifacts docker images list us-central1-docker.pkg.dev/my-project-id/my-docker-repo --format "table(package, version, createTime)"

faq.question: What package formats does Artifact Registry support?
faq.answer: Docker, Maven, npm, Python (PyPI), Go, Apt (Debian), Yum (RPM), and Helm. Each format has its own repository type.

faq.question: How do I clean up old images?
faq.answer: Use cleanup policies: 'gcloud artifacts repositories set-cleanup-policies my-docker-repo --location us-central1 --policy=policy.json' to automatically delete images older than a specified age or keep only the N most recent tags.
